summary: Archived run artifacts that could not safely accept top-level envelope fields now carry embedded archived envelopes, so preserved run history no longer appears as avoidable currentness debt.

observations.problem: Several archived run artifacts were schema-backed but too strict to accept top-level canonical envelope fields, so they kept surfacing as missing-envelope/currentness debt even though they represent preserved historical evidence rather than live operational output.
observations.why_it_matters: When archived run evidence is indistinguishable from current producer debt, artifact-freshness counts stay noisy and maintainers cannot safely reduce stable artifact debt without risking schema-invalid history rewrites.
observations.automation_candidate: Allow an optional metadata.properties extension surface on the strict archived run schemas, embed the canonical archived envelope there, and backfill only the archived run artifacts whose generated_at can be derived deterministically from payload or sibling timestamps.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-28: the safe archived run tranche now backfills changed-files-manifest, planning-validation, promotion-report, and run-ledger with embedded archived envelopes, and generated-contract tests pin both the schema surface and the debt-free freshness outcome.

observations.problem: The archived baseline/candidate mechanism-assessment pair still looks like stable-contract debt, but unlike the safe archived run quartet its legacy generated_at values come from capture-time clocks and now lag file mtime, so envelope-only normalization would erase an unresolved currentness ambiguity.
observations.why_it_matters: If maintainers treat the mechanism-assessment pair as another schema-safe envelope backfill, archive exemption would suppress real mtime drift without proving that the historical assessment timestamps can be reconstructed or normalized consistently across baseline/candidate comparisons.
observations.automation_candidate: Before any backfill helper is widened, define a mechanism-assessment archive contract that either regenerates the pair deterministically from canonical inputs or records an explicit provenance-backed timestamp normalization rule strong enough to replace the original generated_at/currentness semantics.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated by widening the archived-run backfill boundary to the baseline/candidate mechanism-assessment pair with provenance-backed embedded envelopes, focused helper regressions, and checked-in artifact-freshness contract tests that keep the debt-free outcome visible.
